notes: Codex 0.130.0 runs /compact as an ordinary turn appended to the SAME rollout/session as the priming turn (no new transcript at the boundary, unlike claudecode 2.1.x). Live probe (2026-05-23) confirmed: priming prompt emits task_started→task_complete (~1.6s), then /compact emits a second task_started → compacted/context_compacted (skipped by parser) → task_complete (~8.3s). The parser maps task_complete→turn_done, so the daemon sees one clean working→ready arc per turn with no flicker. This spec asserts the compaction produces exactly ONE working→ready arc on the same session, with no spurious waiting and no extra transitions beyond the priming arc and the compaction arc. irrlicht cannot label the turn as a compaction (CompactionState is dead code; context_compacted is skipped), so the compaction-vs-normal-turn distinction is not observable — only the state arc is. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE driver: the live REPL stays alive across both turns so the daemon observes the full working→ready arcs on the rollout session, then process_exited only at REPL teardown. compaction_end anchors to compaction_start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-compaction ready, not the earlier priming ready.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
Priming prompt populates context: ready → working on the rollout session
Priming turn completes back to ready; never entered waiting
/compact runs as a turn: ready → working on the same session
Compaction turn completes back to ready — one clean working→ready arc, no waiting/flicker
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session
